body:
## Summary

- Update `.agent` to depend on `acpx@^0.6.1`
- Refresh the lockfile so workflow `npm ci` installs `acpx@0.6.1`
- Keep the transitive ACP SDK and zod entries aligned with the newer adapter

## Verification

- `npm ls acpx --package-lock-only`
- `npm test`

Closes #69

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #71 for issue #69; an automated review is the appropriate next step before merge.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777569675893 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNzkyNzAyODQ6aW1wbGVtZW50OjY5OnJldmlldzo3MToz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body closes #69; implementation matches linked issue diagnosis and requested lockfile refresh. Issue comment explicitly notes `npm ci` remains pinned without lockfile update. |
| coding / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff touches only `.agent/package.json` and `.agent/package-lock.json`, changing `acpx` from `^0.4.0` to `^0.6.1` plus required transitive lockfile entries. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Fix uses the existing `.agent` npm dependency path consumed by workflow `npm ci`; no workflow glue or custom runtime workaround was added. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is the minimal dependency and lockfile update needed to move runtime installs from `acpx@0.4.0` to `acpx@0.6.1`. |

## Notes

- Main score driver: the PR directly addresses the reported compatibility issue with the smallest dependency-surface change.
- `keep-docs-in-sync` was not scored because the existing docs describe that workflows install `acpx` from `.agent/package.json`, not a pinned version or Claude `permissions.defaultMode` compatibility contract.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific issues found. The PR body reports `npm ls acpx --package-lock-only` and `npm test`; I did not see a rubric gap requiring additional implementation beyond the dependency and lockfile update.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #71 fixes the Claude review lane setup failure reported in #69.
- The failure was tied to `acpx@0.4.0` rejecting current Claude Code `permissions.defaultMode: "auto"` settings.
- The PR updates `.agent/package.json` to require `acpx@^0.6.1` and refreshes `.agent/package-lock.json` so workflow `npm ci` installs `acpx@0.6.1`.
- This matches the linked issue diagnosis and keeps the change limited to the dependency surface needed for the fix.

## Review
Both reviewer inputs agree that the PR is surgical and satisfies the reported compatibility fix. No reviewer raised a blocking or warning-level issue, and no new inline comments were needed.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues found | INFO | Reviewers found the dependency and lockfile update appropriate. Reported verification includes `npm ls acpx --package-lock-only` resolving `acpx@0.6.1` and `npm test` passing. |

## Progress
- PR body documents `npm ls acpx --package-lock-only` and `npm test`.
- Rubrics review passed with no required follow-up.
- Existing PR discussion already records the automation handoff and rubric result.
- Existing inline PR comments were checked; none existed, so no duplicate feedback was at risk.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: No unresolved actionable branch changes remain.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #71.

no rubric changes

PR #71 and linked issue #69 did not contain trusted maintainer feedback warranting a new or updated rubric. The PR conversation was bot-only, and the human linked-issue comments from `lolipopshock` were marked `CONTRIBUTOR`; they describe a specific dependency fix rather than an endorsed durable workflow/style preference. Existing rubrics already cover the relevant general lessons: read linked context, make surgical changes, reuse existing code, and keep implementations simple.

title: Update acpx for Claude auto permission mode
